title: Clausius-Clapeyron Vapor Pressure Analyzer
description: Stepped-temperature experiment that measures vapor pressure at 8 discrete temperatures (30–65 °C in 5 °C steps) inside a sealed borosilicate vessel. A BMP280 barometric sensor reads headspace pressure; a DS18B20 measures bulk liquid temperature. A resistance heater steps temperature and allows 5-minute equilibration per point. On-board Arduino performs a Clausius-Clapeyron linear regression (ln P vs 1/T) and reports ΔHvap to Serial and SD card.
objective: Determine the enthalpy of vaporization ΔHvap of a pure liquid (e.g., ethanol or water) from 8 stepped-temperature pressure measurements and a least-squares Clausius-Clapeyron fit.
constraints: Arduino Uno, DS18B20, BMP280, sealed glass vessel ≤ 3 bar, 12V resistance heater; no volatile flammable solvents near open flame; max vessel temperature 70 °C.
design_element_titles: Sealed Glass Reaction Vessel with Pressure Port

element_usage_notes.DS18B20 1-Wire Temperature Probe Array: Immersed in liquid to confirm equilibrated temperature before pressure is recorded.
element_usage_notes.BMP280 Barometric Pressure Sensor Module: Reads headspace pressure after thermal equilibration at each temperature step.
element_usage_notes.Sealed Glass Reaction Vessel with Pressu: Maintains fixed liquid volume; pressure port connects BMP280 to headspace.
element_usage_notes.Resistance Heater with PWM Control: Bang-bang control steps temperature to each target; held until equilibration.
element_usage_notes.SD Card Data Logger Module: Logs all (T, P, ln P, 1/T) rows plus final ΔHvap result.

element_interactions.to: Sealed Glass Reaction Vessel with Pressure Port
element_interactions.description: Heater warms liquid in sealed vessel to each target temperature step
Pressure sampled only after temperature drift < 0.1 °C/min
Equilibrated pressure logged with corresponding temperature
Temperature logged alongside pressure for regression dataset
